findings.finding_text: Gate could reject valid historical source carry-forward objects if the strict canonical requirement is applied uniformly. Mitigation: allow an explicit historical source allowlist and only apply the strict canonical requirement to latest snapshot references by default.

New gate could duplicate existing cross-surface byte parity code. Mitigation: reuse lovs.cross_surface_parity.check_cross_surface_parity() and keep the new module focused on snapshot JSON plus canonical asset comparison.
Running the gate against the current dirty local website will fail. This is expected and useful; the gate should report it as local-review-branch-blocked, not as a code failure.

Daily prep could become too expensive if it runs the full website suite. Mitigation: this change adds only the hard Python gate and preserves the existing focused website suite; expanding to the full suite can be a follow-up.
